commands.review_summary:
The branch moves the existing built-in `/new` command definition ahead of `/help` in `buildBuiltinChatCommands` without changing the command metadata.

PR surface: Source 0. Total 0 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug: source inspection and Mantis before/after Telegram Desktop proof give a high-confidence demonstration of the current and proposed command order.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; maintainers need to decide whether `/new` should replace `/help` as the first default native command, then wait for exact-head required checks before merge.

target.title: feat(commands): move /new to the top of native command menu
target.body:
Move the /new command (start a new session) to be the first entry in the built-in native command registry, making it appear first in Telegram/Discord bot command menus.

/new is by far the most frequently used command in daily workflows. Placing it first reduces friction for users who reach for it constantly, similar to how Hermes prioritizes it.

Before: help, commands, tools, ..., reset, new, compact, ...
After:  new, help, commands, tools, ..., reset, compact, ...

## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

- Problem:
- Solution:
- What changed:
- What did NOT change (scope boundary):

## Motivation

Explain why this change should exist now. Link it to the user pain, failure mode, maintainer need, or product goal. If this is purely mechanical, write `N/A`.

-

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature

- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

- [ ] Integrations

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

External contributors must show after-fix evidence from a real OpenClaw setup. Unit tests, mocks, lint, typechecks, snapshots, and CI are supplemental only. Screenshots are encouraged even for CLI, console, text, or log changes; terminal screenshots and copied live output count. Be mindful of private information like IP addresses, API keys, phone numbers, non-public endpoints, or other private details when providing evidence.

- Root cause:
- Missing detection / guardrail:
- Contributing context (if known):

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:

```text
Before:
[user action] -> [old state]

After:

```
